reasoning: The question revolves around whether the WHO will declare a new coronavirus pandemic (specifically keyed to a bat virus like the newly discovered HKU5-CoV-2) by December 31, 2025. As of late October 2025 (the knowledge cutoff), there has not been a single verified human case of HKU5-CoV-2, as confirmed by multiple authoritative, scientific, and news sources published throughout 2025 up to at least August. For a virus to cross over to humans, spread globally, and be officially declared a pandemic by the World Health Organization normally takes many months, even after a large regional outbreak is underway (for example, COVID-19 took over two months from the first reports to a pandemic declaration). Given that there are zero known human cases with just over two months remaining in the year, it is virtually impossible for a completely new global coronavirus pandemic to emerge and be formally declared by the WHO before December 31, 2025. The probability is therefore set to the lowest constrained bound.
